definition:
1. as preposition, 
2. with genitive loci, outside, before , παρὲκ λιμένος (Odyssey by Homer); παρὲξ ὁδοῦ out of  the road, (Iliad by Homer) 
3. like χωρίς, besides, except, exclusive of , παρὲξ τοῦ ἀργύρου (Herdotus Historicus) 
4. with accusative out by the side of, along side of , παρὲξ ἅλα (Iliad by Homer); παρὲξ τὴν νῆσον away from  the island, (Odyssey by Homer); παρὲξ δοῦρα out of the way of  spears, (Odyssey by Homer); παρὲκ νόον out of  sense and reason, foolishly, (Iliad by Homer); παρὲξ Ἀχιλῆα without the knowledge of  Achilles, (Iliad by Homer) 
5. as adverb, 
6. of Place, out beside , στῆ δὲ παρέξ hard by , (Iliad by Homer); νῆχε παρέξ was swimming out along shore , (Odyssey by Homer) 
7. metaphorically beside the mark , παρὲξ ἀγορεύειν (Iliad by Homer) 
8. ἄλλα παρὲξ μεμνώμεθα let us talk of something else , (Odyssey by Homer); παρὲξ ἢ ὅσον except  so long as, (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)
